title: acer negundo subsp. typicum
intro: Acer negundo subsp. typicum, also known as the boxelder, is a subspecies of the boxelder tree native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Acer negundo subsp. typicum include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and act as an antiseptic. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of Acer negundo subsp. typicum include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from its leaves, bark, and roots, which are used to treat various health issues.
